text.title: sql-optimization Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the sql-optimization methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer adds 5 indexes 'to be safe' without an EXPLAIN baseline; write latency triples.
antipattern.fix: Drop unjustified indexes; gate every CREATE INDEX on an EXPLAIN ANALYZE diff.

antipattern.symptom: Team optimizes a nightly batch query (10s once a day) instead of the 100ms-x-10K/day path that dominates p95.
antipattern.fix: Use slow-query log + pg_stat_statements to rank by total_time = mean_time * calls; optimize top-N.

antipattern.symptom: Optimized query still SELECT *; covering index never kicks in.
antipattern.fix: Replace SELECT * with explicit column list aligned to the covering index.

antipattern.symptom: Bulk load of 10M rows; optimizer keeps old stats; new query uses a seqscan instead of the new index.
antipattern.fix: Run ANALYZE table_name after the bulk load; verify with EXPLAIN.
